connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: T CM CO2 Saved (Tn)
fields.tableau_formula:
AVG(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [co2_saved_tonnes]
END)

fields.caption: T CM Capacity Factor
fields.tableau_formula:
AVG(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [capacity_factor_actual]/100
END)

fields.caption: T CM Homes Powered
fields.tableau_formula:
AVG(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [homes_powered_annually]
END)

fields.caption: T CM Performance Ratio
fields.tableau_formula:
AVG(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [performance_ratio]/100
END)

fields.caption: T CM Total Capacity
fields.tableau_formula:
SUM(IF MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [capacity_mw]
END)

fields.caption: T CM Total Output
fields.tableau_formula:
SUM(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [energy_actual_mwh]
END)

fields.caption: Spiral 20s
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) IN (100,200,300,400,500) THEN [Calculation_1072419677151514642]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) IN (100,200,300,400,500) THEN [Calculation_1072419677151596563]
END

)

fields.caption: Spiral Check
Spiral Axis Guides
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(
CASE [Num]
WHEN 1 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 2 THEN 2  // Up
WHEN 3 THEN -2  // Down
WHEN 4 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 5 THEN 0  // Right
WHEN 6 THEN 0 // Left
END

,

CASE [Num]
WHEN 1 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 2 THEN 0  // Up
WHEN 3 THEN 0  // Down
WHEN 4 THEN 0  //Centre
WHEN 5 THEN 2  // Right
WHEN 6 THEN -2 // Left
END

)

fields.caption: Max Monthly Output
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169563484176] = WINDOW_MAX([CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169563484176])
THEN 'Max'
END

fields.caption: Month Label
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = MONTH([date])
THEN LEFT(DATENAME('month', [date]),1)
ELSE LEFT(DATENAME('month', [date]),1)
END

fields.caption: Selected Turbine ID
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [turbine_id]
END

fields.caption: Min Monthly Output
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169563484176] = WINDOW_MIN([CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169563484176])
THEN 'Min'
END

fields.caption: Month in View
fields.tableau_formula:
IF MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
THEN DATENAME('month', [date]) + ' 2024'
ELSE '2024'
END

fields.caption: T Neg MoM Total Output
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169676390478] < [PM Total Output (copy)_2281636169676390476]
THEN ([CM Total Output (copy)_2281636169676390478]/[PM Total Output (copy)_2281636169676390476])-1
END

fields.caption: T PM Total Output
fields.tableau_formula:
SUM(IF (MONTH([date]) = [Parameters].[Parameter 1]-1
OR [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024)
AND [upd_turbine_name] = [Parameters].[Upd Turbine Name Parameter]
THEN [energy_actual_mwh]
END)

fields.caption: Selected Month (Bars)
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = MONTH([date])
THEN 'Selected Month'
ELSEIF [Parameters].[Parameter 1] = 2024
THEN 'Annual'
ELSE 'Others'
END

fields.caption: Spiral First Point
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) = 0 THEN [Calculation_1072419677151514642]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) = 0 THEN [Calculation_1072419677151596563]
END

)

fields.caption: Spiral Last Point
fields.tableau_formula:
MAKEPOINT(

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) = MIN([Calculation_1072419677154189337]) THEN [Calculation_1072419677151514642]
END

,

CASE TRUE
WHEN MIN([Num]) = MIN([Calculation_1072419677154189337]) THEN [Calculation_1072419677151596563]
END

)

theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

Extract-based source (no live connection). All 4 relationship-model tables materialized from the packaged .hyper to import-mode CSVs: Turbine (30), Daily Performance (10,980=30x366), CO2 Savings (30), NL Densification (126 Year/Num spiral scaffold). Modeled as a star: Turbine (dim) 1->* Daily Performance and 1->* CO2 Savings on Turbine Id; NL Densification disconnected.

Parser emitted null `formula` for all 87 calculated fields + 8 parameters. Recovered from the .twb <calculation formula='...'> XML (95 formulas) and used as the translation source of truth. Internal names are heavily Ctrl-drag-scrambled (e.g. internal 'CM CO2 Saved (Tn) (copy)' -> caption 'CM Homes Powered'); dispositions keyed on parser-resolved captions, never internal names.

-> disconnected slicer table 'Month Parameter' (Month Number/Month/Month Order) + measure [Month Parameter Value] = SELECTEDVALUE('Month Parameter'[Month Number], 6). Default 6=June baked in so a bare EVALUATE reproduces the default dashboard state. 2024='24 means annual/all-year. Gates every CM/PM measure (the P1 month filter).

-> slicer table 'Page Parameter' + [Page Parameter Value] (default '1'). Backed Tableau's show/hide-sheet page navigation; in Power BI page navigation is native (buttons/bookmarks), so retained only as selection domain. The Page 1..6 boolean calcs are NOT materialized (bookmark/nav concern).

-> single-row seed table 'Radius Parameter' + [Radius Parameter Value] = SELECTEDVALUE(...,500). Spiral base-radius tuning knob held at the Tableau default; can be upgraded to a what-if slider for interactive spiral tuning.
-> single-row seed table 'Spiral Start Point' + [Spiral Start Point Value] = SELECTEDVALUE(...,180). Spiral start-angle (degrees) knob at Tableau default.
-> single-row seed table 'Thickness' + [Thickness Value] = SELECTEDVALUE(...,0.1). Spiral radius-increment knob at Tableau default.
-> slicer table 'Region Parameter' + [Region Parameter Value] (default 'Flevoland', 5 NL provinces). NOT referenced by any calculated field; exposed as a native region slicer domain.
-> slicer table 'Turbine Id Parameter' + [Turbine Id Parameter Value] (default 'NL-WT-2024-001', 30 ids). NOT referenced by any calculated field; exposed as a native turbine-id slicer domain.

-> disconnected single-select slicer table 'Upd Turbine Name Parameter' + [Upd Turbine Name Parameter Value] (default 'GRWF Turbine 18', 30 turbines). Drives every 'T ...' turbine-specific measure and the spiral length (parameter-equality idiom, SELECTEDVALUE equivalent).

Spatial MAKEPOINT([X],[Y]) - a CARTESIAN spiral point, NOT geography. Disposition: render as a native scatter/line visual driven by measures [Spiral X]/[Spiral Y] over the NL Densification Num index (higher fidelity than a map). The MAKEPOINT geometry object itself is not materialized (no DAX equivalent); the underlying cartesian coords are exposed as measures instead.
Spatial MAKEPOINT decorative marker at Num in (100,200,300,400,500). Disposition: report-layer annotation (reference points on the spiral scatter). Not materialized as a model field.
Spatial MAKEPOINT decorative up/down/left/right axis guide markers. Disposition: report-layer annotation. Not materialized.
Spatial MAKEPOINT(0,0) centre marker. Disposition: provided as constant measures [Spiral Zero X]=0 / [Spiral Zero Y]=0 for a single origin reference point on the scatter.
Spatial MAKEPOINT of the spiral's first point (Num=0). Disposition: report-layer annotation (first point derivable from [Spiral X]/[Spiral Y] at Num=0). Not materialized.
Spatial MAKEPOINT of the spiral's last point (Num=MIN([Max Path])). Disposition: report-layer annotation (last point = [Spiral X]/[Spiral Y] at Num=[Spiral Length]). Not materialized.
NO MAKELINE / great-circle geometry anywhere in the workbook - therefore NO arc/route capability gap. The only real geography (Map worksheet) is turbine point locations.

Real turbine geography (latitude/longitude on turbine_master_data, plotted by the 'Map' worksheet). Disposition: kept as columns on the Turbine dim, tagged dataCategory Latitude/Longitude (+ Region=StateOrProvince) so the report binds them to a native azureMap point/bubble layer (30 turbines).
Table calc INDEX(). Disposition: positional index = report-visual sort/rank concern, not materialized as a model field.
Table calc RANK([CM Total Output],'desc'). Disposition: measure [Rank] = RANKX(ALL('Turbine'[Upd Turbine Name]), [CM Total Output], , DESC, Dense) on Turbine.

limitations_encountered.item: field.Max Monthly Output / Min Monthly Output
limitations_encountered.issue: Table calcs WINDOW_MAX/WINDOW_MIN over the month axis. Disposition: measures [Max Monthly Output]/[Min Monthly Output] using MAXX/MINX over ALLSELECTED('Daily Performance'[Month]) returning the 'Max'/'Min' label (evaluate with Month on the axis).

LOD FIXED [upd_turbine_name]:SUM(FIXED [upd_turbine_name],[Num]: IF [Spiral Filter] THEN 1 END) - 1 (per-turbine spiral point count). Disposition: simplified to measure [Spiral Length] = INT([T CM Performance Ratio (Abs)]) - with integer Num, count of Num in [0,abs] minus 1 = INT(abs). Verified = 104 at default.

limitations_encountered.item: field.Spiral Check / Spiral Filter / Spiral Colour
limitations_encountered.issue: Num-index gating calcs referencing the scalar [T CM Performance Ratio (Abs)]. Disposition: measures on NL Densification. [Spiral Colour] adds an explicit NOT ISBLANK guard because DAX BLANK<=100 evaluates TRUE (would flip Tableau's 'B' to 'A').

limitations_encountered.item: field.Selected Turbine / Selected Turbine ID / Selected Month / Selected Month (copy) / Selected Month = 2024 / Selected Page / Page 1-6
limitations_encountered.issue: Parameter-equality idiom (IF [Parameters].[X] = [Dim] ...). Disposition: NOT materialized as calculated columns; replaced by native slicers/bookmarks on the parameter tables. Only [Selected Turbine ID] is kept as a convenience measure (returns the selected turbine's turbine_id).

limitations_encountered.item: field.Selected Month (Bars) / Month in View / vs. PM Label / in 2024 Label / Month Label
limitations_encountered.issue: Param-dependent label fields. Disposition: implemented as measures (they depend on the live Month slicer, so cannot be static calc columns). [Month Label] IS a calc column - its Tableau IF/ELSE branches were identical (dead conditional), simplified to unconditional LEFT(month,1).
Constants. Disposition: measures [Country]="Netherlands", [Today]=TODAY().
Decorative constants (180 / 'info'). Disposition: not materialized (layout-only, no analytical value).

4 worksheet-local decorative calc ids (radial 'Months' arc, e.g. Calculation_2281636170049314942) exist only in worksheet shelves, not in the datasource field list. Disposition: report-builder decorative concern; not modeled.

SOURCE BUG: Tableau's [T PM Capacity Factor] omits the /100 that [T CM Capacity Factor] applies, so [T *MoM Capacity Factor] compares a fraction (~0.29) to a percent (~35.6) - a 100x scale mismatch (T Neg MoM CapFac ~ -99%). Translated exactly as authored and flagged; NOT silently 'fixed'.

limitations_encountered.item: field.CM Total Capacity / T CM Total Capacity
limitations_encountered.issue: SOURCE ISSUE: Tableau SUMs capacity_mw over month-filtered DAILY rows, fan-out-inflating by the day count (e.g. June = 30x130.2 = 3,906 MW); [T CM Total Capacity] additionally has an AND/OR precedence bug (missing parens). Implemented as sensible STATIC nameplate (fleet SUM = 130.2 MW; per-turbine = selected capacity) and flagged, since a capacity KPI returning 3,906 MW is unusable.

SOURCE BUG: AND/OR precedence (missing parens) -> 'MONTH=P1 OR (P1=2024 AND name=param)'. For a specific month it is NOT turbine-filtered (returns the fleet mean); only turbine-specific when annual (P1=2024). Translated faithfully as IF(P1=2024, <selected turbine>, <fleet mean>) and flagged.

CO2 is ANNUAL per turbine, so [T CM CO2] = [T PM CO2] always -> T MoM CO2 is definitionally 0 (Neut) / blank (Pos,Neg). Translated faithfully and flagged as a data-grain characteristic (no monthly CO2 breakdown exists).

limitations_encountered.item: field.CM CO2 Saved (Tn) / CM Homes Powered / CM Trees / T CM CO2 / T CM Homes
limitations_encountered.issue: CO2/homes/trees are ANNUAL per turbine; the Tableau MONTH([date]) gate over the daily-joined rows is month-INVARIANT (all 30 turbines have complete 366-day data, so every month weights turbines equally). Implemented as AVERAGE over CO2 Savings (fleet) / selected-turbine CALCULATE - proven to equal the Tableau value (fleet default = 5,634.38). The month gate is a structural no-op.

limitations_encountered.item: field.Total Fleet Capacity / No of Turbines / Active Turbines / Onshore Turbines
limitations_encountered.issue: Fleet-summary KPI tiles not present as datasource calc fields but shown on the dashboard header. Provisioned as measures on Turbine ([No of Turbines]=30, [Active Turbines]=28 Operational, [Onshore Turbines]=24, [CM Total Capacity]=130.2) so the report has backing fields.

Tableau [Radius]/[Angle]/[X]/[Y] renamed to [Spiral Radius]/[Spiral Angle]/[Spiral X]/[Spiral Y] to avoid confusion/ambiguity with the 'Radius Parameter'[Radius] column and single-letter names. All references updated.

TmdlSerializer.DeserializeDatabaseFromFolder OK: 12 tables, 2 relationships. Integrity asserted programmatically: 75 measures all uniquely named model-wide; no measure name equals a column name in the same table; every DAX [bracket] reference resolves to a real measure/column.

16/16 numeric ground-truth checks vs the extracted CSVs PASSED, incl. the parameter-driven [CM Total Output] at its Month=6/June default = 28,240.79, [T CM Total Output] (GRWF Turbine 18, June) = 1,117.32, [T CM Performance Ratio (Abs)] = 104.68 -> [Spiral Length] = 104, [Total Actual Output (2024)] = 453,167.28, [Total Co2 Saved] = 169,031.37, [CM CO2 Saved (Tn)] = 5,634.38.

Copilot-readiness pass (offline via powerbi-modeling-mcp ConnectFolder -> *_operations Update description -> ExportToTmdlFolder to definition/ -> Disconnect). Business-meaning descriptions now cover 100% of the model: 12/12 tables, 58/58 columns, 75/75 measures. Descriptions lead with meaning + unit/grain (MWh additive, % 0-100 average, m/s, C, kV, MW, m); DAX Copilot reads the first 200 chars. Table & measure descriptions pre-existed as TMDL /// doc comments from the build; this pass added the 58 column descriptions. Verified: partial Update preserved dataType/dataCategory/summarizeBy/annotations (Latitude/Longitude/StateOrProvince dataCategory intact for azureMap). Re-validated: DeserializeDatabaseFromFolder OK, 12 tables / 2 rels / 75 unique measures, no /// on relationships.

Every categorical/dimension column now lists its domain (enum) values in its description so Copilot can resolve natural-language filters. Enumerated: Turbine[Onshore Offshore]={Onshore,Offshore}; [Region]={Flevoland,Friesland,Groningen,Noord-Holland,Zeeland}; [Manufacturer]={Enercon,GE Renewable,Nordex,Siemens Gamesa,Vestas}; [Model]={Cypress 5.3,E-115,N149-4.5,SG 5.0-145,V136-4.2,V150-4.2}; [Operational Status]={Operational,Maintenance}; [Owner]={Dutch Wind Consortium,Eneco,Essent,Nuon,RWE,Vattenfall}; [Grid Connection Voltage Kv]={33,66,110,150}; [Capacity Mw]={3.5,4.2,4.5,5.0,5.3}. Daily Performance[Month]={January..December}; [Weekday]={Monday..Sunday}. Slicer domains: Month Parameter[Month]={Jan..Dec,'24}; Region/Turbine Id/Upd Turbine Name/Page parameter tables enumerate their selectable values + baked default.

SKIPPED (not committable in PBIP/TMDL today): AI data schema. Authored via LSDL with no stable Git/file contract, so it cannot be reliably round-tripped through the definition/ TMDL folder. Recommend setting it in the Fabric service / Desktop after deploy.
SKIPPED (not committable today): AI instructions (model-level Copilot guidance). LSDL-authored, no stable file contract in the TMDL folder. Set post-deploy in the service.
SKIPPED (not Git-supported): verified answers / linguistic Q&A pairs. Not persisted in the committable TMDL definition; configure in the service after publish.

SKIPPED (service/tenant setting, not a model artifact): 'Approved for Copilot' / semantic-model indexing toggles. These are workspace/service settings applied after deployment, out of scope for the local PBIP build.

Placed as native slicers per report handoff, but their SELECTEDVALUE measures have 0 consumers in the committed model (a documented Tableau parameter->DAX gap inherited from semantic_build) - changing them filters nothing. Region is a display-only domain; Turbine Id is inert.

Shows all 30 turbines (fleet locator). The disconnected Upd Turbine Name Parameter cannot cross-filter the Turbine table, so the map cannot isolate the single selected turbine; selection is reflected only in the T-* KPI cards and spiral.

Displays FLEET total actual output, not selected-turbine output - the model exposes no day-level turbine-filtered output measure. Faithful to the Tableau fleet heatmap but does not react to the turbine slicer.
Marker color is static teal. Tableau's 'Spiral Colour' encoding is a string measure (returns a color/label) which cannot drive PBIR data-color rules; per-point color fidelity is lost.
Weekday axis sorts alphabetically - the model has no Weekday sort-order column and the report layer must not modify the semantic model. Minor ordering fidelity loss on the heatmap rows.

Non-data Tableau zones omitted: Capacity/Owner label, Months headline, Turbine Image (no asset shipped), Page-navigation buttons, and the decorative spiral markers (Spiral 20s / Axis Guides / First / Last / Zero point). These are annotations, not data visuals.

Tableau's 5-page, 6-turbines-at-a-time pager (Page Parameter) collapsed into ONE natively-scrolling tableEx turbine list. Native scroll supersedes the page-flip control; the paging buttons have no faithful native equivalent.

limitations_encountered.item: control.Selected Month vs All Year toggle
limitations_encountered.issue: The Tableau 'Selected Month / All Year' toggle (P1=2024 member) is collapsed into the Month slicer default (June) plus a Month-in-View card; the annual view is reachable by selecting the 2024 member of the Month slicer.

Live Desktop render verification deferred: the single shared Power BI Desktop bridge was held by two other concurrent builds (one with unsaved changes) and per policy a competing instance was not force-opened. Sign-off rests on structural validation (powerbi-report-author validate: 0 errors) plus an independent field-reference cross-check (32/32 refs resolve against TMDL). Spiral tracing and azureMap bubble rendering are structurally verified but not yet visually confirmed; PBIR_SCHEMA_UNREACHABLE (offline) means JSON-schema validation was skipped.
